contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Was having dinner with Jerry just now in Palo Alto after meeting Nora up north in Berkeley… (_literally looped the entire Bay Area at this point_)
  
  Seems to have landed on the same conclusion, using ramen as the parallel analogy. 
  
  Don’t try to replace the chef making the ramen using an LLM, the RnD cost of discovering all the possible parameters to what makes a good bowl of ramen far outstrips the total ever possible amount of revenue that could be generated selling bowls of ramen.
  
  The same is true for chocolate. 
  
  Otherwise the other aspects of the entire operations/supply chain is very very old and traditional and the constrains already known. You don’t really need a cutting edge frontier model for the ops. Even DeepSeek can be used to model the whole ops to streamline its management 
  
  K&L wine mystery box is a good prior art to reference for our monthly mystery box for chocolate bars
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh, Jerry Luk
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: IMG_2616.png
- Destination Contribution File Location: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/.github/tree/main/assets/contribution_20260614055806_gary_teh_img_2616.png
